title: Mental Health Apps Under Scrutiny
subtitle: 心理健康应用真的有效吗？专家提出质疑

paragraphs.en: The mental health app market has exploded in recent years, offering everything from meditation exercises to AI-powered therapy chatbots. Millions download these applications seeking relief from anxiety, depression, and stress. Yet scientific evidence supporting their effectiveness remains surprisingly limited.
paragraphs.zh: 心理健康应用市场近年来呈爆发式增长，提供从冥想练习到AI驱动的治疗聊天机器人等各种服务。数百万人下载这些应用程序，寻求缓解焦虑、抑郁和压力。然而，支持其有效性的科学证据仍然出奇地有限。

paragraphs.en: Researchers point out that most mental health apps reach the market without rigorous clinical testing. Unlike pharmaceutical drugs, digital therapeutics face minimal regulatory requirements before public release. Users essentially become unwitting participants in uncontrolled experiments with their psychological wellbeing.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员指出，大多数心理健康应用在没有经过严格临床测试的情况下就进入市场。与药品不同，数字疗法在公开发布前面临的监管要求很少。用户实质上成为了在不受控制的实验中拿自己心理健康做实验的不知情参与者。

paragraphs.en: Privacy concerns add another layer of complexity to the debate. Mental health apps collect extraordinarily sensitive personal information about users' emotional states, thoughts, and behaviors. Data breaches or commercial exploitation of this intimate information could cause serious harm.
paragraphs.zh: 隐私问题为这场辩论增添了另一层复杂性。心理健康应用收集关于用户情绪状态、思想和行为的极度敏感的个人信息。这些私密信息的数据泄露或商业利用可能会造成严重伤害。

paragraphs.en: Proponents argue that apps can democratize access to mental health support. Traditional therapy remains expensive and inaccessible for many people, while smartphones are nearly universal. Even imperfect digital tools might help those who would otherwise receive no support at all.
paragraphs.zh: 支持者认为，应用程序可以使心理健康支持的获取更加民主化。传统疗法对许多人来说仍然昂贵且难以获得，而智能手机几乎人人都有。即使是不完美的数字工具，也可能帮助那些否则根本无法获得支持的人。

paragraphs.en: The path forward likely requires both innovation and regulation. Establishing standards for evidence-based digital mental health tools would protect consumers while encouraging legitimate development. Users should approach these apps with informed skepticism—as supplements to professional care rather than replacements for it.
paragraphs.zh: 前进的道路可能需要创新和监管并重。建立循证数字心理健康工具的标准将保护消费者，同时鼓励合法的开发。用户应该以知情的怀疑态度对待这些应用——将它们作为专业护理的补充而非替代品。
